title: gentianella amarella subsp. uliginosa
intro: Gentianella amarella subsp. uliginosa, also known as marsh gentian, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the gentian family, characterized by its small yellow flowers and preference for growing in wet, boggy areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The health benefits of marsh gentian include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine to make a syrup or salve for topical application.
